config_file.description: Optional list of YAML config files to load.

name.description: Display name shown in the GUI tree and reports.

skipped.description: If truthy, the step is skipped (static expression, evaluated at load time).

key.description: Report key used to classify the result (typically <test>_PASS or <test>_FAIL).

stop_on_failure.description: If true, abort the surrounding container on failure.

execute_on_stop.description: If true, run this step even when its container is being stopped (cleanup).

process_result.description: Post-evaluation expression applied to the test result.

store_result.description: Global-dict key in which to store the test result.

expected_result.description: Expected outcome; the step is failed if it doesn't match.

no_fail.description: If truthy, never report a FAILURE for this step.

condition.description: Optional gating expression evaluated before each run; false ⇒ the step is skipped.

seq_filename.description: (internal) source .tum file of this step; injected by the loader.

test_file.description: Path to the Python unittest file (TestCase subclass).

test_method.description: Optional list of method names to restrict the run to. When empty, every test_* method in the file is run.

test_file.description: Path to the pytest test file.

write_delay.description: Inter-character write delay in ms (slow devices).

log.description: Path to a log file capturing the console traffic.

overwrite_log.description: If true, truncate the log file at open; else append.

ssh_pwd.description: SSH password (if key-based auth is not used).

tcp_port.description: TCP port for a raw-TCP connection.

buffered.description: If true, the serial console buffers received bytes between reads.

terminal_path.description: Working directory for the local terminal protocol.

shell.description: Shell command used for the local terminal protocol (default: 'cmd.exe' on Windows, '/usr/bin/env bash' elsewhere).

expected.description: Literal string — or a list of strings — matched against the incoming console output. The read succeeds as soon as one of them is seen, or fails on timeout.

timeout.description: Seconds before giving up. Negative means infinite.

mute.description: If true, don't echo received bytes to testium's stdout/log.

regex.description: If true, each 'expected' entry is treated as a Python regular expression (searched, not anchored) instead of a literal string.

iterator.description: Iterable (or string expanding to one) driving the loop. The current value is exposed as $(loop_param).

exit_condition.description: Optional block stopping the loop early: combine 'time', 'value', or a 'file'+'func_name' pair (with optional 'param' and 'eval').

file.description: Path to the .py file containing the function.

func_name.description: Name of the function to call in the file.

param.description: Arguments passed to the function. Each entry is expanded before the call. Special tokens $(loop_param) / $(loop_index) resolve from the surrounding cycle.

context_id.description: If set, the py_func subprocess is kept alive and reused by every other py_func item with the same context_id — enables shared in-memory state between successive calls.

process_result.description: Post-evaluation expression applied to the test result.

store_result.description: Global-dict key in which to store the test result.

expected_result.description: Expected outcome; the step is failed if it doesn't match.

no_fail.description: If truthy, never report a FAILURE for this step.

condition.description: Optional gating expression evaluated before each run; false ⇒ the step is skipped.

seq_filename.description: (internal) source .tum file of this step; injected by the loader.

tum.description: Path to the .tum file launched in a fresh testium instance.

param_file.description: Optional path to a param.yaml passed to the sub-instance.

log_file.description: Path where the sub-instance writes its log.

report_file.description: Path where the sub-instance writes its report.

start_time.description: HH:MM time of day after which the sub-instance may run.

end_time.description: HH:MM time of day after which the sub-instance no longer runs.

wait_for_exec.description: If true, block until the time window opens. Requires both start_time and end_time.

batch.description: Run the sub-instance headless (-b) with its output captured into this test's log/report and result value, even in the GUI.

name.description: Display name shown in the GUI tree and reports.

skipped.description: If truthy, the step is skipped (static expression, evaluated at load time).

key.description: Report key used to classify the result (typically <test>_PASS or <test>_FAIL).

stop_on_failure.description: If true, abort the surrounding container on failure.

execute_on_stop.description: If true, run this step even when its container is being stopped (cleanup).

params.description: Parameters payload (list, dict or scalar) sent to the method.

id.description: JSON-RPC request id. 'rand' (default) ⇒ a random integer is used.

no_wait.description: If true, send the request without waiting for a response.

timeout.description: Seconds to wait for a response. None ⇒ inherits the transport default.

console.description: Console-transport block: {name, …} (name = console_name of an open console). Either 'console' or 'udp' must be set.

udp.description: UDP-transport block: {server, snd_port, rcv_port, …}. Either 'console' or 'udp' must be set.

timeout.description: Default seconds to wait for a JSON-RPC response across all child query/receive actions.

mute.description: If true, don't echo wire traffic to the log.

name.description: Display name shown in the GUI tree and reports.

skipped.description: If truthy, the step is skipped (static expression, evaluated at load time).

branches.description: List of branch blocks (each branch holds its own 'steps' and optional 'wait_for').

sync.description: 'all' (default) waits for every branch; 'any' returns as soon as the first branch completes.
